q: A rigid body is a body that does not deform or change ___.
In a circular motion, the object just moves in a ___.
The equations for constant angular acceleration in rotational motion are analogous to equations for constant acceleration in ___ motion.
All particles of an object rotating around the axis of rotation move in a ___ path.
The relation between arc length $s$ and angular displacement $\theta$ is given by the equation ___.
The instantaneous ___ $\omega$ of rotational object is the same for all the particles comprising the object.
The linear velocity is also called ___ velocity.
The tangential acceleration $a_T$ relates to angular acceleration $\alpha$ by the equation ___.
The direction of the ___ vector is perpendicular to the plane of rotation, and along the axis of rotation.
The direction of angular velocity is usually specified by the ___ rule.
When the angular velocity is increasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ direction as the angular velocity.
When the angular velocity is decreasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ to the angular velocity.
An object that moves in a circle at constant speed $v$ is said to experience ___ circular motion.
The change in direction of velocity constitutes an ___.
Acceleration $\vec{a}$ is in the same direction as $\Delta \vec{v}$, it also points toward the ___ of the circle.
Centripetal acceleration is also denoted by ___.
The centripetal acceleration $a_c$ can be obtained as ___.
For uniform circular motion, the linear velocity vector is ___ to the path.
Centripetal acceleration and linear velocity are ___ to each other at every point in the path.
In ___ circular motion, an object is moving in a circular path with a varying speed.
In nonuniform circular motion, the angular speed of the object is ___ and the object experiences the angular acceleration.
In nonuniform circular motion, there is ___ acceleration in addition to centripetal acceleration.
Unlike tangential acceleration, centripetal acceleration is present in ___ uniform and nonuniform circular motion.
The two acceleration vectors $\vec{a}_c$ and $\vec{a}_T$ are ___ to each other.
The resultant acceleration points at an ___ between $\vec{a}_c$ and $\vec{a}_T$.
The magnitude of resultant acceleration $\vec{a}$ is ___.
The direction of resultant acceleration $\vec{a}$ is given by ___.
If the initial angular speed is $\omega_0$, its angular speed $\omega$ at time $t$ can be derived from the following constant angular acceleration equation ___.
The relation between the magnitude of the linear velocity $v$ and the angular velocity $\omega$ of a particle is ___.
Average angular speed is ___.
___ is a body that does not deform or change shape.
In ___, the object just moves in a circle.
In rotational motion, the object rotates about ___.
The distance between any two particles within a rigid body remains ___ no matter how it moves.
In rotational motion, all the particles constituting the rigid body undergo ___ about a common axis.
If the angular velocity of an object changes at a constant rate, the motion is called ___.
a: rotational motion under constant angular acceleration

q: The average angular speed $\bar{\omega}$ is given by the equation ___.
The angular displacement for time interval $t$ can be expressed as ___.
The equations for constant angular acceleration are analogous to equations for constant acceleration in ___ motion.
The kinematic equation relating $v$ and $v_0$ is $v = v_0 + at$. Its angular counterpart is ___.
The conversion between revolution and radian is $1\text{ rev} = $ ___.
All particles of an object rotating around the axis of rotation move in a ___.
The relation between arc length $s$ and angular displacement $\theta$ is ___.
The instantaneous angular velocity $\omega$ of a rotational object is ___ for all the particles comprising the object.
The relation between the magnitude of the linear velocity $v$ and the angular velocity $\omega$ is ___.
In the equation $v = r\omega$, the angular velocity $\omega$ is measured in ___.
Since the direction of linear velocity is tangent to the path, the linear velocity is also called ___.
If the magnitude of the angular velocity changes, the object will have a ___ acceleration.
The direction of tangential acceleration is always ___ to the circular path.
Tangential acceleration $a_T$ relates to angular acceleration $\alpha$ by the equation ___.
The tangential acceleration of a rotating object is a measure of how fast the ___ changes.
The direction of the angular velocity vector is ___ to the plane of rotation.
The direction of angular velocity is usually specified by the ___.
When using the right-hand rule, wrap the right hand around the axis of rotation so that the fingers point in the direction of ___.
The thumb points in the direction of the ___ when applying the right-hand rule.
When the angular velocity is increasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ direction as the angular velocity.
When the angular velocity is decreasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ direction to the angular velocity.
Artificial satellites going around the earth at a constant height is an example of ___ motion.
Rotation of earth about its own axis creates the cycle of ___.
The motion of the blades of a fan is an example of ___.
